purpose: Small, source-justified predictor sets for fixed-window macro forecasting experiments.
notes: Columns with *_delta or macro mnemonics generally come from data/processed/stationary_panel.parquet unless marked derived.
T10Y3M_level is derived as GS10 - TB3MS from levels_panel.parquet; T10Y3M_delta is its first difference.
These feature sets are intentionally small and defensible, not automatically optimized.

conference_board_lei.title: The Conference Board Leading Economic Index for the US
conference_board_lei.relevance: Lists leading indicators such as manufacturing hours, initial claims, new orders, building permits, stock prices, interest-rate spread, and consumer expectations.

fed_ip_methodology.title: Federal Reserve Industrial Production and Capacity Utilization Methodology
fed_ip_methodology.relevance: Explains that IP uses monthly production indicators and, when physical output is unavailable, input measures such as production-worker hours.

fed_capacity_utilization.title: Federal Reserve Capacity Utilization Notes
fed_capacity_utilization.relevance: Defines capacity utilization as output relative to sustainable maximum output, closely tied to industrial activity.

stock_watson_diffusion.title: Stock and Watson (2002), Macroeconomic Forecasting Using Diffusion Indexes
stock_watson_diffusion.relevance: Justifies using broad monthly macro panels and reduced predictor sets/factors for macro forecasting.

rba_labor_indicators.title: RBA Bulletin: How Useful are Leading Labour Market Indicators at Forecasting the Unemployment Rate?
rba_labor_indicators.relevance: Motivates leading labor-market indicators for unemployment-rate forecasts.

features.name: UNRATE_level
features.reason: Current unemployment level captures strong persistence in unemployment-rate forecasts.

features.name: UNRATE_delta
features.reason: Recent month-to-month unemployment change captures labor-market momentum.

features.name: CLAIMSx
features.reason: Initial unemployment claims are a direct leading labor-market stress indicator and an LEI component.

features.name: HWIURATIO
features.reason: Help-wanted to unemployed ratio proxies labor demand relative to available workers.

features.name: PAYEMS
features.reason: Payroll employment growth is a coincident labor-market condition and helps anchor unemployment movements.

features.name: PERMIT
features.reason: Building permits are a cyclical leading indicator; weaker housing activity can precede weaker labor demand.

features.name: S&P 500
features.reason: Stock prices summarize forward-looking expectations and are an LEI component.

features.name: T10Y3M_level
features.reason: Yield-curve slope is a business-cycle leading indicator; inversions often precede unemployment deterioration.

features.name: UMCSENTx
features.reason: Consumer expectations are an LEI component and can anticipate spending/hiring weakness.

features.name: INDPRO_level
features.reason: Current industrial production level captures persistence and business-cycle state.

features.name: INDPRO_growth
features.reason: Recent IP growth captures production momentum.

features.name: IPMANSICS
features.reason: Manufacturing IP is a major component of aggregate industrial production.

features.name: CUMFNS
features.reason: Capacity utilization measures output relative to sustainable capacity and is directly tied to industrial activity.

features.name: AWHMAN
features.reason: Average weekly hours in manufacturing are used as an LEI component and production-worker hours are an input to some Fed IP estimates.

features.name: AMDMNOx
features.reason: Manufacturers' new orders proxy forward production demand and are an LEI component.

features.name: CMRMTSPLx
features.reason: Real manufacturing and trade sales are part of coincident business-cycle conditions and demand for production.

features.name: PERMIT
features.reason: Building permits lead construction-related materials and durable-goods demand.

features.name: S&P 500
features.reason: Stock prices summarize forward-looking financial expectations for activity and demand.

features.name: T10Y3M_level
features.reason: Term spread summarizes monetary/financial conditions relevant for investment, goods demand, and production cycles.
